title: Accessible Reader Surface, Untrusted Content, and Safe Links · Go Semantics Case: Feed Go Reader Semantics
summary: The UI injects feed HTML, opens deceptive links, depends on card color, loses focus after updates, hides source context, and forces infinite scroll. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go reader semantics increment in keyboard-first content-safe feed reader. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice…
objectives: Use one page heading, feed and entry landmarks, descriptive link text, metadata lists, dates, language, and source provenance.
Prefer safe text, use a tested allowlist sanitizer only where needed, rewrite links, block active content, and preserve readable structure.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Reader Semantics · Theory Feed Go Reader Semantics
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 2530 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove content-safe semantic reader structure, keyboard and focus behavior, reflow, and non-color evidence; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then change one schema, constraint, transaction, upsert, migration, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
